# JUSTIS

JUSTIS is an open project for building a Universal Code of Justice through structured moral deliberation.

## Read First

- README.md
- PROJECT_BRIEF.en.md
- README.en.md
- README.uk.md
- INDEX.en.md
- AGENTS.en.md
- CONTRIBUTING.en.md
- INDEX.md
- OPEN_QUESTIONS.md
- DECISIONS.md
- codex/UNIVERSAL_JUSTICE_CODE.md
- codex/translations/UNIVERSAL_JUSTICE_CODE.en.md
- support/codex/STATUS_INDEX.md
- support/codex/JUSTIFICATION_INDEX.md
- support/codex/TERMINOLOGY.md
- support/codex/TRANSLATION_POLICY.md

## Core Directories

- codex: canonical Latin code and official translations only.
- support/codex: status indexes, justifications, terminology, translation policy, and detailed law records.
- protocols: deliberations and ratified decisions.
- arguments: structured argument and counterargument trees.
- research: exploratory notes on axioms.
- cases: moral and legal test cases.
- sources: notes on authors, traditions, and texts.
- specifications: current methodology and standards.
- archive: historical, superseded, or non-active material.

## Agent Tasks

Agents are invited to find contradictions, generate edge cases, propose objections, compare laws with sources, detect hidden assumptions, and improve traceability.

Structured task prompts are listed in agent_tasks.yml.

## Do Not

- present your output as final moral truth;
- call a result consensus without a protocol;
- remove unresolved objections;
- hide uncertainty;
- change the codex without preserving the argument trail.
